segments.text: This lamp has no sensors and no batteries. Yet, [music] it knows exactly when to refill itself. You can call this magic dia, and it uses this clever physics trick. So, here is the mystery.
You [music] pour oil into this top container, which holds about 200 ml.
Logically, the gravity [music] should pull all the oil down and flood the tray immediately, creating a huge mess. But it does not. It stops. The oil only
drops when the flame [music] burns it away. It works on atmospheric pressure.
The moment oil level drops, an air bubble enters, releasing just enough oil to fill it back up. But how do you make a vacuum seal with just a mud? See, if
the clay is porous, it leaks. But that's the genius [music] of this terra cotta art. They find special clay, knead it for days, and bake it at,000°C.
It turns simple earth into a machine that lasts forever. [music] Ancient tech was just built different. Subscribe for more.
